item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He told his wish…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sonofabitch

item.title: Excellent job
item.content:encoded:
Man, I was not expecting to be hit so hard by this. You captured the decline of Ed's memory and the ending was quite heartbreaking.

+1, and it's well-earned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh no. Oh no, oh no, oh no.

That's exactly it. That is literally… This is even sadder now. Really incredible article, especially for a second entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gah the fridge horror and brilliance made my mind splatter all over my room.

I need to upvote this now.(And I usually never vote at all :) )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god. I knew things were only gonna get worse after reading examination log 2, and…. That last examination… hit me way harder than it should have. Coupled with what undergroundmonorail said above, this whole skip is just super tragic to me. Just the whole concept of slowly losing memory or knowledge just hits super close home to me.

Feels induced +1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you monster D:

Dammit, now my tentative upvote is a strong uovote. I hadn't even considered this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait

did the old man's personality go away because he told someone what his birthday wish was

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In that case, you could always keep it as is with an explanatory footnote. I didn't realize it was a real system, you certainly needn't reduce realism on my behalf.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a roleplay character on World of Warcraft whose backstory is almost this. A grown woman who accidentally reversed time around herself and turned herself into a child, but with all the mental faculties and intelligence of her grown-up self, that slowly and steadily whittled away until her mind snapped back to match her body. I called the effect the 'dissolution of temporal dissonance' or something silly like that.

There are few anomalies on this site that I would really call favorites, but this… this anomaly resonates with me on a very strong level. It will definitely go down as one of my favorites in recent memory. Even if I didn't have that silly connection to make with this, I would still have greatly enjoyed the slow feel-trip I was taken on with this poor old man.

Excellently done! I'd +2 if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looking at it from the outside, this is a kid under the delusion that he's a sixty-year-old man.

I think part of the anomaly is that the kid retains the personality and memories of a person the kid would have no way of knowing existed. I think the first log also implies that the Foundation was just "investigating" anomalous activity during the first interview, since the doc's clearly skeptical. Maybe some more bitey proof was discovered O.S.?

But the intelligence tests are just letters and numbers with no meaning; it took me well past the halfway point to realize I could even glean information from them.

FWIW, didn't confuse me all that much. It's easy to see the numbers are getting lower as time goes on, but I can't speak for anyone else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MA/CA is actually a real thing, though I think it's fallen out of favor since I was a kid. The result is actually MA ÷ CA × 100, so 100 is the "normal." I'm going to consider changing it since it bothered you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops, sorry, double post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't intend a connection. I honestly just pulled "Project SAMSARA" out of thin air, not thinking to google it or anything to see if I was stealing somebody else's name.

item.title: A round of applause
item.content:encoded:
If this is your second god damn scip I am extremely excited for what is to come.

Downvoted and upvoted to give you a +2 good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this article. Its sad, but hopeful if that makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. I mean, I like the story that's told, full stop. But the intelligence tests are just letters and numbers with no meaning; it took me well past the halfway point to realize I could even glean information from them. And "guy reincarnates as kid" just seems so… pedestrian? I mean, yeah, that's an anomaly, it's anomalous, but I nevertheless found myself wondering why the Foundation would even care. Looking at it from the outside, this is a kid under the delusion that he's a sixty-year-old man. And he slowly loses that delusion. It just seems hard to call this an anomaly worth containment. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The MA/CA ratio numbers don't really make sense. A 1 (i.e. 1:1) would signify the same mental age as physical age. A 10 would signify a 10:1 ratio, I.E. a mental age of 60 in a 6 year old.

The current number doesn't appear to be a ratio at all. I like the skip but I couldn't stop thinking about that the whole way through. The current numbers would work well if you just threw in a decimal, i.e. 8.04 instead of 804.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is true, but I am not sure that the author didn't intend a connection here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the number of articles that have been and will continue to be produced, I imagine this is going to happen now and again. No canon is best canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Project SAMSARA was the Foundation program to use the technology they inherited from Prometheus Labs along with the LoE cell cultures and gestalt personalities composited from the destructive brain scans of dying special operators.

I guess it could be a different "project SAMSARA", but that seems confusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a different SAMSARA, apparently. Project, as opposed to MTF.

Rebirth and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not all authors put out spoilers/explanations, but it's definitely not frowned upon when they do. Many who do will either put 'em in a spoiler-marked collapsible in the comments, post them in the forum thread titled The Leak, or both.

So post as thou wilt!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the relevance to SAMSARA, candidates for the neural gestalts at that stage were special operators. Why would they be looking at little kids?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, I'm just being deliberately speculative. I think the amount you reveal in the SCP is perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you want me to tell you? I'm very new and don't know the general attitude toward pulling back the curtain. I'll tell you if you want to know, assuming it's not a faux pas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: am i the only one who can see the fuckin sunrise here? this breaks the world, k

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. The memory loss is scary, but I'm more concerned with what happened to the original Lonnie…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of your mind slowly fading has always been something that horrifies me, and this captures it in a solidly done way. I started to realize what was happening before I got to the end and the whole way through after it was painful to read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FUCK YEAH this is good. I had a good feeling the second I opened that first collapsible, the custom formatting. When things started clicking it HURT.

I really hope you stick around & show us more where that came from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is your second goddamn skip what the fuck how are you this good at it already

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn, I felt really bad for the subject.

Immortality does not come without a price.
